questions.questionText: Koebner phenomenon (isomorphic response) is seen in all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.optionB: Pemphigus vulgaris (Koebner is NOT a feature of pemphigus — it is seen in psoriasis, lichen planus, vitiligo, warts, molluscum)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Koebner = skin lesions appearing at sites of trauma/injury in susceptible individuals. Why correct: Classic Koebner diseases: Psoriasis, Lichen planus, Vitiligo, Viral warts, Molluscum contagiosum. Pemphigus does NOT show Koebner. Why A wrong: Psoriasis — classic Koebner positive. Why C wrong: Vitiligo — depigmentation at trauma sites. Why D wrong: LP — papules at scratch sites. Clinical relevance: Reverse Koebner: clearing of lesions at trauma site (psoriasis can show this too). Pathergy: exaggerated skin response to minor trauma — Behçet's disease, pyoderma gangrenosum. Exam trap: Pathergy ≠ Koebner. Pathergy = pustule at needle prick (Behçet's). Koebner = disease-specific lesion at trauma. Memory aid: 'Koebner = P-L-V-W-M (Psoriasis, LP, Vitiligo, Warts, Molluscum).' Reference: IADVL Textbook – General Dermatology.

questions.optionB: Psoriasis (pinpoint bleeding on scraping off silvery scales — due to dilated capillaries in dermal papillae close to thinned suprapapillary epidermis)
questions.explanation: Core concept: Auspitz sign = pinpoint bleeding when psoriatic scale is removed = pathognomonic. Why correct: Psoriasis — well-defined erythematous plaques with silvery scales. Grattage test: candle-grease sign (waxy scale) → Auspitz sign (bleeding points) → last membrane sign. Bleeding due to elongated dermal papillae with dilated capillaries close to surface. Why A wrong: LP — Wickham's striae (fine white reticular lines on papules). Why C wrong: Pemphigus — Nikolsky sign positive (skin peels with lateral pressure). Why D wrong: DH — grouped vesicles on extensors, IgA deposits (linear). Clinical relevance: Psoriasis signs: Auspitz, candle-grease, Koebner, Woronoff ring (blanching around resolving plaques). Histology: Munro micro-abscesses (neutrophils in stratum corneum). Exam trap: Nikolsky sign positive in pemphigus vulgaris AND TEN/SSSS. Auspitz is ONLY psoriasis. Memory aid: 'Auspitz = Aus-scratch-Psoriasis-bleeding.' Reference: IADVL Textbook – Psoriasis.
questions.conceptTag: Auspitz sign psoriasis pinpoint bleeding scale
